stories.headline: A FIGHT WITH THE HOSTILES; BIG FOOT'S TREACHERY PRECIPITATES A BATTLE. CAPT. WALLACE OF THE SEVENTH CAVALRY KILLED, WITH MANY INDIANS AND SOLDIERS -- LIEUT. GARLINGTON SERIOUSLY WOUNDED.
stories.summary: Captain Wallace of the Seventh Cavalry was killed in a battle with Native American hostiles, with many Indians and soldiers also killed or wounded, including Lieutenant Garlington, during a time of heightened tensions between the US government and Native American tribes

stories.headline: PARNELL SPLITS HIS PARTY; THE MAJORITY DRIVEN FROM HIM BY HIS SELFISHNESS. JUSTIN M'CARTHY ELECTED LEADER OF HIS OPPONENTS -- THE OLD LEADER LEFT WITH A FEW ADHERENTS AND THE CONTROL OF THE MONEY. BY THE COMMERCIAL CABLE FROM OUR OWN CORRESPONDENT.
stories.summary: Charles Parnell's leadership was challenged by Justin McCarthy, who was elected to lead the opposing faction, amid a split in the party and questions about Parnell's character

stories.headline: BIG STRIKE IN SCOTLAND; RAILROAD TRAFFIC STOPPED AND BUSINESS CHECKED. NINE THOUSAND MEN STOP WORK -- VERY FEW TRAINS RUNNING -- FUEL SCARCE AND THE SUPPLY OF GAS THREATENED.
stories.summary: A large strike by railroad workers in Glasgow, Scotland, brought railroad traffic to a halt and threatened the supply of fuel and gas, as 9,000 men stopped work

stories.headline: FOUR BLOCKS LAID WASTE; LONDON LOSES AT LEAST TWO MILLIONS BY FIRE. A LARGE NUMBER OF WAREHOUSES DESTROYED -- A BIG LOSS IN SEALSKINS ALONE -- THE USELESSNESS OF HAND ENGINES SHOWN.
stories.summary: A massive fire in London's Queen Victoria Street destroyed a large number of warehouses, including those storing valuable seal skins, and caused significant financial losses

rounds.blurb: Captain Wallace's death occurred during a battle with Native American hostiles. The Seventh Cavalry clashed with Big Foot's tribe, resulting in many casualties. Tensions between the US government and Native American tribes escalated.

stories.headline: LANDED ON ELLIS ISLAND; NEW IMMIGRATION BUILDINGS OPENED YESTERDAY. A ROSY-CHEEKED IRISH GIRL THE FIRST REGISTERED -- ROOM ENOUGH FOR ALL ARRIVALS -- ONLY RAILROAD PEOPLE FIND FAULT.
stories.summary: A new immigration building on Ellis Island has opened, with a young Irish girl being the first to register, during a time of high immigration to the United States. The facility is expected to handle all arrivals efficiently.

stories.headline: ENGLAND'S NOBLE DEAD; MANNING'S FUNERAL TO FOLLOW THE YOUNG DUKE'S. TO BE MADE VERY IMPRESSIVE -- RUMORS OF PEACE IN ITALY BETWEEN CHURCH AND STATE -- ONE OF BISMARCK'S FRIENDS IN TROUBLE -- BULGARIA THE ALLIED POWERS.
stories.summary: The funeral of a young duke is to be held in England, and will be a grand affair, as the country mourns the loss of its noble dead. Meanwhile, rumors of peace between the church and state in Italy are emerging.

stories.headline: SHERMAN MEN ARE VICTORS; A SHARP FIGHT. BUT THEY NOMINATE LAYLIN AS SPEAKER. THE FORAKER FACTION SURPRISED AND DOWNCAST -- SHERMAN'S ELECTION TO THE UNITED STATES SENATE PRACTICALLY ASSURED.
stories.summary: John Sherman's supporters have won a significant victory, nominating Laylin as speaker and practically assuring Sherman's election to the United States Senate, in a contest against Foraker's faction.

stories.headline: EUROPE'S EYE ON GERMANY; THE EMPEROR FACE TO FACE WITH LIBERAL OPPOSITION. A SITUATION THAT WAS TO BE YORE- SEEN -- FRENCH DEVOTION TO GER- MAN PEER -- AS TO POPE LEO'S HEALTH -- DUKE CONSTANTINE'S DEATH -- THE GRIP STILL IN FORCE.
stories.summary: The German Emperor is facing opposition from liberal forces, in a situation that has been brewing for some time, and is being closely watched by European powers, as the Emperor's luck in politics seems to be running out.

rounds.blurb: The Ellis Island immigration building opened, efficiently handling arrivals with a young Irish girl as its first registrant. The funeral of a young English duke was held with great pomp. John Sherman's supporters secured a significant victory, nominating Laylin as speaker.

stories.headline: CREW OF CRIPPLED APOLLO 13 STARTS BACK AFTER ROUNDING MOON AND FIRING ROCKET; MEN APPEAR CALM DESPITE LOW RESERVES
stories.summary: The crew of Apollo 13 is heading back to Earth after successfully rounding the moon and firing a rocket, despite low reserves, as the United States continues to push the boundaries of space exploration during the Cold War era. The astronauts appear calm and in control of the situation.

stories.headline: NIXON TO PULL OUT 150,000 FROM VIETNAM IN A YEAR; SAYS HANOI BLOCKS PEACE
stories.summary: President Richard Nixon announced plans to withdraw 150,000 troops from Vietnam over the next year, citing Hanoi as an obstacle to peace, in a televised address to the nation from his San Clemente home. The move is seen as a significant step towards ending the conflict.

stories.headline: DEMOCRATS PICK GOLDBERG BUT HE ‘WAIVES’ SELECTION; PLANS A RACE BY PETITION;
stories.summary: Democrats selected a candidate, but he declined the nomination and instead plans to run through a petition process, adding a new layer to the election. The candidate, Goldberg, is moving forward with his campaign despite the initial selection.

stories.headline: Judge Blackmun of Minnesota Is Named To Supreme Court Seat by the President
stories.summary: Judge Blackmun from Minnesota has been appointed to the Supreme Court by the President, marking a new addition to the nation's highest court. The appointment is a significant move, shaping the judicial landscape.

rounds.blurb: Apollo 13's crew successfully rounded the moon and fired a rocket, heading back to Earth despite low reserves. President Nixon announced plans to withdraw 150,000 troops from Vietnam. Judge Blackmun was appointed to the Supreme Court by the President.
